key_evidence: First baseline. Illinois is absent from site/src/data/indexes/us-states.json (21 of 51 jurisdictions present); no prior composite exists, so score_delta is null.

rank is null by design: the published index lost ranks 9-38 in the legacy-HTML extraction and renumbered survivors contiguously, so displayed rank is not national rank. Inserting Illinois at any position would assert a false ordering.

Systemic Thinking 4.0 is the only dimension at or above 4.0 and is what lifts the composite: CEJA directs 0M/yr to clean-energy jobs in Black and Brown communities; R3 permanently dedicates 25% of cannabis tax revenue and has distributed 30M+ to 300+ organizations; 780,000+ cannabis records cleared leaving 94 people incarcerated on cannabis offenses.

Integrity 3.2 and Accountability 3.2 are held down by documented conduct, not reputation: Madigan sentenced to 7.5 years on 2025-06-13, no major ethics bill in 4+ years, 2,310 corruption convictions 1983-2023; B.H. child welfare decree unresolved after three decades and Lippert prison health decree failing after seven years.

Integrity conflict scored explicitly: Illinois closed Health Benefits for Immigrant Adults on 2025-07-01 (30,000+ people lost coverage) in the same period it filed 50+ suits against federal action and litigated Operation Midway Blitz. I1 scored 3, not higher.
Boundaries 2.8 is the weakest dimension: 1,108 of 1,342 Ligas class members selected in July 2023 were still unserved as of March 2024; state-documented shortfall of 4,236 shelter beds.
Composite 60.0 verified with computeCompositeFromDimensions (site/scripts/lib/scoring.mjs). Base 60.0, stdDev 0.38, 7 of 8 dimensions below 4.0 so weaknessFactor 0 and integrationPremium 0.
Peer calibration: above New York 56.9, Pennsylvania 54.4, Delaware 53.8, Michigan 51.9, Ohio 38.1, Georgia 26.9. Margin over New York is 3.1 points and rests entirely on SYS.
